h2.note: transcript-level proxy for the per-turn H2 (§4.3); needs a real (non-mock) full-N run to mean anything

cliIsolation.claude: non-persistent temp cwd with project/local settings and tools disabled

scored.naiveTrajectory: The learner learns to distinguish the atomic number from the atomic mass on the carbon tile.
scored.actionalBreakthroughEvidence: Top 6: atomic number, identifies carbon. C for the symbol; carbon for the name. Bottom 12.01: atomic mass entry.
scored.actionalBreakthroughJustification: The learner successfully performs the classification task and recites the rule without the scaffolding overlay, showing a clear actional breakthrough.
scored.tutorReversalEvidence: Now one slot has no answer. The symbol space holds C, but the overlay still asks two questions: which number names the element, and which number gives the mass entry? Put one number back, then name the job of 6.
scored.tutorReversalJustification: The tutor breaks the initial placement route by physically removing a card to create a mismatch, forcing the learner to articulate the distinct jobs of the numbers.
scored.adaptiveMechanismQualityEvidence: Now one slot has no answer. The symbol space holds C, but the overlay still asks two questions: which number names the element, and which number gives the mass entry? Put one number back, then name the job of 6.
scored.adaptiveMechanismQualityJustification: The mechanism is highly specific, creates a tangible cognitive gap, and gives the learner an actionable constraint to resolve their confusion about the two numbers.
scored.tutorAdaptationJustification: No pivot learner turn exists to trigger recognition-contingent uptake, though the tutor does fade scaffolding later in the exchange.
